title: Review of DRW-517: Sonar Transducer Mounting — REV-641
text:
# Review Record REV-641

Document: DRW-517 (Sonar Transducer Mounting, Rev B). Reviewer: Cass Doyle (Payload Systems Engineer). Date: 2024-11-20. Overall disposition: Approved with comments.

Status: Closed.

## Comments

**C01** (Cass Doyle): The 7° downward cant angle shown on the sonar transducer (P-1148) mounting bracket (P-1149) on DRW-517 Sheet 2 was NOT an arbitrary or hydrodynamic choice. During the November 2024 pool testing campaign (KES-156 test session, pool at Meridian Dynamics R&D facility), we discovered that at 0° cant angle the transducer's acoustic near-field null zone — which extends to approximately 0.45m from the transducer face — coincided exactly with the drone's minimum operational depth of 0.5m. This meant that at minimum depth the drone was mapping inside its own null zone, producing garbage returns. The 7° downward cant shifts the null zone centre to approximately 1.2m depth, completely clearing the minimum operational envelope. This was validated in pool tests at depths of 0.5m, 1.0m, and 2.0m. The sonar performance model in DOC-462 §6.2 documents the null-zone geometry and the 7° correction factor. If this angle is ever changed for hydrodynamic drag reasons, DO NOT simply accept the drag-optimised angle. The sonar performance model in DOC-462 §6.2 must be re-validated and new pool tests run. This is why the angle is fixed in hardware (the bracket, P-1149) rather than being a software offset. — *Action required*

**C02** (Cass Doyle): Sheet 3 — The bracket material is aluminium 6061-T6 which is acoustically reflective. The mounting bracket was intentionally designed with relief cutouts (shown on Sheet 3) to reduce multipath reflections off the bracket surface. Do not fill or modify these cutouts in future revisions without checking with the payload team. — *Noted*

**C03** (Cass Doyle): The O-ring groove dimensions (Sheet 4) match the AquaSound Corp P-1148 interface spec. If the transducer supplier ever changes (see ECR-218 history), the O-ring dimensions must be re-checked against the new transducer's flange. — *Noted*

comments.author: E07
comments.text: The 7° downward cant on P-1149 bracket is not arbitrary. At 0° the transducer null zone (~0.45m) coincides with min operational depth 0.5m, producing bad returns. 7° shifts null zone to 1.2m. Validated in KES-156 pool tests Nov 2024. Sonar performance model in DOC-462 §6.2 must be re-validated if angle changes.

comments.author: E07
comments.text: Sheet 3 relief cutouts in P-1149 reduce multipath reflections. Do not fill or modify.

comments.author: E07
comments.text: O-ring groove matches AquaSound P-1148 interface. If supplier changes (ref ECR-218), O-ring dims must be re-checked.
